title: USDA-NIFA Organic Transitions Program (Award No. 2022-51106-37926)
funder: USDA-NIFA (PI: Dr. Gabriel Maltais Landry; Co-PI: Dr. Mathews Paret)
description_short: Multi-year field study evaluating regenerative practices across Conventional and Organic Carrot-Peanut-Corn rotations in Florida. Quantified disease severity via AUDPC demonstrating regenerative management suppressed Alternaria leaf blight in conventional systems.
highlights: Demonstrated cumulative disease suppression via AUDPC in conventional Carrot-Peanut-Corn rotations; Identified Gibberella ear rot transitional risks in corn; Provided preliminary field evidence of soil suppressiveness against Rhizoctonia solani in carrots; Responsible for experimental design, pathogen data collection, statistical analysis, and annual federal reporting

title: California Fresh Carrot Advisory Board — Fungicide Chemigation Trial
funder: California Fresh Carrot Advisory Board (CFCAB) (PI: Dr. Mathews Paret)
description_short: First-of-its-kind field efficacy trials comparing chemigation (solid-set overhead sprinkler irrigation) vs. tractor-mounted sprayer application for Alternaria leaf blight control on carrots. Results directly informed fungicide management protocols for ~86% of U.S. carrot production.
highlights: First study comparing chemigation vs. tractor application for carrot fungicide delivery; Results adopted by Grimmway Farms for ~50,000 acres of commercial production; Findings submitted to Crop Protection (under review 2026); Integrated UAV-based DJI multispectral data (NDVI/NDRE) for disease severity quantification

title: Seed Pathology & Inoculum Dynamics — Commercial Carrot Seed Survey
description_short: Comprehensive four-year survey of commercial carrot seed lots using multi-locus phylogenetic analysis (ITS, GAPDH, Alt a 1) to characterize seed-borne fungal pathogens. Experience evaluating antifungal efficacy of conventional fungicides, biological controls, and a fixed quaternary ammonium copper (FQ-Cu) hybrid nanoparticle.
highlights: Identified Alternaria alternata as predominant seed-borne pathogen via multi-locus phylogenetics; Knowledge of FQ-Cu hybrid nanoparticle evaluation as seed treatment; Used Python for 4PL regression modeling of in vitro EC50/EC90 sensitivity; Skills in ISTA freeze-blotter and standard germination protocols
